message:
Fix uint32_t overflow in load percentage calculations

Extract percent_load_u32() helper that widens to uint64_t before the
multiply-then-divide to prevent overflow on large values. adapter.c also
extracts scale_capacity_for_class() to handle R1/R3 capacity scaling
with saturation instead of overflow. Tests add UINT32_MAX edge cases.
The beads issue tracks this bug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2n0.5","title":"Implement crash/restart replay continuity package for persisted scenarios","description":"Background:
Real systems restart and can lose power mid-write; semantic continuity across restart boundaries is critical for trust.

Subtasks:
- Define persisted trace and snapshot continuity contract.
- Implement restart replay validation for representative failure points.
- Add power-loss simulation scenarios for partial write, torn frame, and interrupted flush boundaries.
- Build artifact collection for continuity failures with detailed logging.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Restarted replay preserves canonical semantic digest where contract requires.
- Continuity tests cover partial-write, power-loss, and interrupted-run edge cases.
- Gate integrates into CI hard quality checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:51.054271490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:27:41.332361754Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:27:41.332343730Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-7","reliability","replay"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.15","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:06:08.614680934Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:51.054271490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:51.756655415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2n0.6","title":"Implement hindsight nondeterminism logging ring and flush-on-failure path","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.H introduces hindsight logging to capture only external nondeterministic inputs with low overhead.

Subtasks:
- Implement bounded ring buffer for nondeterministic boundary events (for example external IO readiness and signal arrivals).
- Flush ring contents on invariant failure or explicit debug trigger.
- Integrate flushed artifacts into replay and first-divergence diagnostics.
- Add tests proving low steady-state overhead and complete divergence context on failure.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same deterministic scenario remains digest-stable with hindsight logging enabled.
- Failure artifacts include enough context to reconstruct divergence.
- Ring capacity and flush policy are configurable and documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.088451867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:23:22.428259299Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:23:22.428240164Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hard-part","replay","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.088451867Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:40.244478265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2n0.7","title":"Implement multi-tier telemetry modes with semantic-neutral guarantees","description":"Background:
Idea round 3 calls for forensic, ops-light, and ultra-minimal telemetry tiers without semantic drift.

Subtasks:
- Define telemetry tiers and retained event fields per tier.
- Ensure canonical semantic digest computation remains independent of telemetry tier.
- Add fixtures comparing behavior across tiers under identical scenarios.
- Document operational tradeoffs and recommended defaults by profile/resource class.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Telemetry tier changes alter observability cost, not runtime semantics.
- Tier mismatch tests prove digest equivalence on shared fixtures.
- Operators can select tier per deployment with explicit guidance.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.170822656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T19:28:00.233950267Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T19:28:00.233931902Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["embedded","ops","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:19.170822656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2n0.7","depends_on_id":"bd-2n0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:40.408017241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-32t","title":"Fix overflow-prone load percentage math in runtime overload/adapters","description":"Deep audit found repeated load_pct/core_pct computations using (used * 100u) / capacity in runtime adapter/overload code. This can overflow uint32_t before division and misclassify overload states. Replace with widened uint64_t arithmetic and add/adjust tests to lock behavior.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"CodexAudit","created_at":"2026-03-01T04:52:51.530508769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T04:52:53.758236801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3vt","title":"EPIC: Advanced Alpha Levers and Research Backlog (No Feature Loss)","description":"Background:
The plan includes advanced ideas (formal assurance ladder, lock-free alternatives, cache-oblivious layouts, etc.). These should be pursued via controlled spikes with rollback and proof artifacts.

Goal:
Run high-EV research tasks that can elevate performance/assurance without semantic compromise.

Constraints:
- one lever at a time
- explicit baseline/profile/isomorphism artifacts
- fallback path mandatory

Why this serves users:
- keeps project ambitious without destabilizing the core delivery path.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.632150816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:41.462963973Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alpha","optimization","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt","depends_on_id":"bd-66l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:14:43.612984800Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.1","title":"Pilot formal assurance ladder from golden outputs to bounded model checks to translation validation","description":"Background:
The plan calls for replacing missing compile-time guarantees with layered assurance artifacts.

Subtasks:
- Define staged assurance levels and entry/exit criteria.
- Pilot bounded model checks on selected lifecycle/state-machine components.
- Define lightweight translation-validation checks for critical transformations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Produces concrete recommendation with cost/benefit and integration path.
- Includes fallback plan if tooling cost outweighs value.
- Leaves behind reusable templates for future formal efforts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:41.331882450Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["assurance","formal","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.956578773Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-296.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.861498197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:51:34.410198918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3vt.10","title":"Prototype optional Rust-AST zero-drift extractor for transition and invariant artifacts","description":"Background:
Plan section 6.8.3B allows an optional zero-drift extractor path to reduce manual transcription drift from Rust semantics.

Subtasks:
- Build minimal tooling prototype (Rust AST parser under `tools/`) that emits transition X-macros and invariant schema fragments.
- Compare generated artifacts against manually curated baseline for one subsystem.
- Define promotion criteria, review requirements, and rollback plan if extractor proves brittle.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Prototype demonstrates measurable drift-reduction value on sampled subsystem.
- Generated artifacts pass parity review before any adoption.
- Clear go/no-go decision and risk note are recorded.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.206339461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","research","spec","tooling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1md.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.206291901Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-296.19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:26:04.095517036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3vt.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3vt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:25:24.858534052Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
